flow.scene: Title card with product logo on a dark background.
flow.observations: Clean minimal design with high contrast.
flow.speech: Welcome to the vidlizer demo. Let me show you how it works.

flow.scene: Terminal window in dark mode, cursor at prompt.
flow.action: User types 'vidlizer demo.mp4' and presses Enter.
flow.context: CLI invocation of vidlizer on a local video file.
flow.observations: Font is JetBrains Mono; iTerm2 on macOS.
flow.speech: I'll run it on a local MP4 file.

flow.scene: Terminal showing Rich-formatted progress output with frame count and cost.
flow.action: vidlizer extracts frames, sends them to the vision model, and streams results.
flow.observations: Dedup reduced frame count by 25%, keeping cost low.
flow.next_scene: JSON output file being written to disk.
flow.speech: It's extracting frames now and removing near-duplicates before sending to the model.

flow.scene: Editor showing the generated JSON analysis file.
flow.action: User opens demo.analysis.json; structured flow array is visible.
flow.context: Reviewing the structured output after analysis completes.
flow.observations: Each step has timestamp_s, scene, action, and speech fields populated.
flow.speech: And here's the resulting JSON — every scene, action, and spoken word, timestamped.

flow.scene: Dark slide with GitHub URL and install command.
flow.observations: Call-to-action for viewers to try the tool.
flow.speech: Give it a try — link is in the description.

transcript.text: Welcome to the vidlizer demo. Let me show you how it works.
I'll run it on a local MP4 file.
It's extracting frames now and removing near-duplicates before sending to the model.
And here's the resulting JSON — every scene, action, and spoken word, timestamped.
Give it a try — link is in the description.
